title: Credentials: Support env-var templates in JSON scaffold
summary: Allow credentials.json fields to contain ${ENV_VAR} template references that are resolved at load time, so tokens are never stored as plaintext on disk. Extend the credentials.Loader with a template resolver, track the source of each credential, and expose source information in preflight output.

stakeholder_intent.actor: Setup user provisioning tokens for a golemic project
stakeholder_intent.goal: Reference environment variables in credentials.json so that token values are never written to disk as plaintext
stakeholder_intent.business_value: Eliminates plaintext secret storage in the credentials file, enables CI/CD secret injection via env vars, and provides audit visibility into where each credential was sourced from
stakeholder_intent.trigger: User runs golemic preflight or golemic run, which invokes credentials.Loader.Load
stakeholder_intent.success_outcome: Credentials are resolved from env vars referenced via ${VAR} template patterns in credentials.json; preflight output shows the source (file literal, env var, template-env, or default) of each token

in_scope: Template resolution for ${ENV_VAR} and ${ENV_VAR:default} patterns in credentials.json field values
New internal/template package with a Resolver interface and default EnvResolver implementation
Extension of credentials.Credentials struct to track the source of each token (file literal, direct env, template-env, or default)
Extension of credentials.Loader.Load to resolve templates after reading file values
Preflight output change to surface credential source information in checkCredentials results
Backward compatibility: plain literal values in credentials.json continue to work unchanged
Template resolution respects existing env-var precedence: GOLEMIC_DEV_TOKEN / GOLEMIC_REVIEWER_TOKEN override everything
Error handling for malformed templates, missing referenced env vars, and empty resolved values
out_of_scope: Template support in config.json or other JSON files
Template references to files or external secrets managers
Encryption or at-rest protection of credentials.json
Changes to the scaffolding trigger or CLI flags

preconditions: credentials.json exists at ~/.golemic/<project>/credentials.json and has valid JSON syntax
Referenced environment variables are set in the process environment when Load is called
credentials.Loader is initialized with a non-empty home directory

business_rules.rule: Template format: a template reference is ${VAR_NAME} where VAR_NAME matches [A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*
business_rules.applies_when: any credentials.json field value contains the substring ${
business_rules.outcome: The value is treated as a template subject to resolution; the matched ${...} segment is replaced with the env var value or default

business_rules.rule: Template validation: unclosed ${, empty ${}, or VAR_NAME with invalid characters cause a load error
business_rules.applies_when: a value contains malformed template syntax (unclosed, empty, or invalid var name)
business_rules.outcome: credentials.Loader.Load returns an error describing the malformed template; no token values or env var names are included in the error message

business_rules.rule: Precedence: explicit GOLEMIC_DEV_TOKEN / GOLEMIC_REVIEWER_TOKEN env vars override template-resolved values and file literals
business_rules.applies_when: GOLEMIC_DEV_TOKEN or GOLEMIC_REVIEWER_TOKEN env var is set (even to empty string)
business_rules.outcome: The explicit env var value is used regardless of file content; template resolution is not performed for that field

business_rules.rule: Missing env var: a template referencing an unset env var without a default produces an error
business_rules.applies_when: ${VAR_NAME} references an env var not present in os.LookupEnv and no :default is provided
business_rules.outcome: credentials.Loader.Load returns an error listing the missing environment variable; the env var name is included but no token value is leaked

business_rules.rule: Idempotency: credential loading always reflects the current process environment at call time
business_rules.outcome: Each call re-reads the file and re-resolves templates against the current environment; no caching of resolved values occurs

business_rules.rule: Mixed credentials: a single credentials.json may contain any mix of plain literals and template references across dev_token and reviewer_token fields
business_rules.applies_when: credentials.json fields contain a mix of literal values and ${...} templates
business_rules.outcome: Each field is independently resolved; a literal dev_token and a template reviewer_token are both valid

business_rules.rule: Default fallback: ${VAR_NAME:default_value} uses default_value when VAR_NAME is unset or empty
business_rules.applies_when: a template includes a colon-separated default (${VAR:default}) and VAR is unset or empty
business_rules.outcome: The resolver substitutes the default_value; the default_value may be empty (${VAR:}) which is treated as explicitly allowed

business_rules.rule: Error safety: template error messages must not contain resolved token values or env var values
business_rules.outcome: Error messages include the env var name (if missing) or malformed template description, but never the resolved token or any env var value

decision_tables.name: Token value resolution for a single credentials.json field

interfaces.name: template.Resolver
interfaces.operation: Resolve(value string) (resolved string, source string, err error)

inputs.name: value
constraints: May contain zero or more ${VAR} or ${VAR:default} patterns
Plain literal strings (no ${}) are returned unchanged

outputs.condition: value contains no templates or all templates resolve successfully
outputs.body: Resolved string with all ${...} replaced, and source tag (file_literal, template_env, or template_default)

errors.condition: value contains unclosed ${, empty ${}, or invalid var name characters
errors.message: malformed template reference in credentials.json: <description without token values>

errors.condition: value contains ${VAR} where VAR is not set in environment and no default is provided
errors.message: environment variable VAR referenced in credentials.json is not set

outputs.condition: credentials.json exists with valid JSON and all templates resolve successfully
outputs.body: *Credentials with devToken, reviewerToken populated; source tracking fields set (DevSource, ReviewerSource)

errors.condition: Referenced env var is not set and no default provided
errors.message: environment variable VAR referenced in credentials.json <field> is not set

errors.condition: File does not exist and no env var override is set

outputs.condition: All credentials resolve and tokens are valid
Credentials fail to load or validate
outputs.body: Result{Name: "Credentials", Ok: false, Details: <error message without token values>}

state_changes.precondition: File does not exist; preflight.checkCredentialsScaffolding is invoked
Credentials struct is defined with devToken and reviewerToken string fields

changes.value_source: Computed during Load: one of file_literal, direct_env, template_env, template_default, or unset
Computed during Load: one of file_literal, direct_env, template_env, template_default, or unset

side_effects.description: Preflight checkCredentials result now includes the source of each credential (e.g., 'dev=direct_env, reviewer=template_env') in the Details field when successful
side_effects.idempotency: Output reflects the resolution that occurred during the current Load call; repeated runs may show different sources if environment changed
side_effects.failure_policy: If source tracking fails (unexpected), the credential load itself fails; no partial source information is emitted

security.authentication: No new authentication; uses existing GitHub token validation via gh api user
authorization_rules: Only the process owner can set environment variables used for template resolution
credentials.json file permissions are still validated (must be 0600) regardless of template usage
security.data_classification: Highly sensitive: credential tokens and env var values used for templates must never appear in logs, error messages, or output
audit_requirements: Preflight output shows the source category of each credential (file_literal, direct_env, template_env, template_default, unset) but never the token value
Template resolution errors log the referenced env var name but never its value

integration_points: credentials.Loader.Load calls template.Resolver.Resolve for each field value
preflight.checkCredentialsScaffolding writes template references into credentials.json skeleton
preflight.checkCredentials reads Credentials.Source() accessors for output details
preflight.createCredentialsSkeleton uses the new skeleton template values
architecture_constraints: New internal/template package must not depend on internal/credentials (no circular imports)
template.Resolver interface must be injectable into credentials.Loader for testability
credentials.Credentials struct public API (DevToken, ReviewerToken methods) must remain unchanged
Source tracking fields must be private with public accessor methods (DevSource, ReviewerSource)
Error messages must never contain token values or env var values (existing constraint)
allowed_changes: Add internal/template package with Resolver interface and default EnvResolver
Add DevSource and ReviewerSource fields and accessor methods to credentials.Credentials
Add Resolver field to credentials.Loader and call Resolve during Load
Update credentialsSkeleton in preflight to emit template references
Update preflight.checkCredentials to include source info in Details
Add unit and integration tests for template resolution, mixed sources, and error paths
forbidden_changes: Changing the public method signatures of Credentials.DevToken() and Credentials.ReviewerToken()
Changing the Loader.Load(project string) (*Credentials, error) signature
Adding template support to config.json or any file other than credentials.json
Logging or displaying resolved token values in any output
dependencies: text/template (stdlib, already used in internal/prompt) — may be reused or a simpler regex-based parser may be preferred for single-value resolution
implementation_context.migration_strategy: Existing credentials.json files with plain literal values continue to work without changes. The new scaffold writes template references, but existing files are never overwritten (idempotency). Users who already have literal tokens in credentials.json are unaffected. Users who want to migrate can manually replace literal values with ${VAR} references.
implementation_context.consistency_and_concurrency: Template resolution is a pure function of the file content and process environment at call time. No shared mutable state. Each Load call is independent. Concurrent Load calls from different goroutines are safe as long as the underlying os.LookupEnv is safe (it is in Go).

acceptance_scenarios.title: Env-var templates resolve from scaffold
given: Environment has MY_DEV_TOKEN=ghp_dev_abc and MY_REV_TOKEN=ghp_rev_xyz set
No GOLEMIC_DEV_TOKEN or GOLEMIC_REVIEWER_TOKEN env vars are set

acceptance_scenarios.title: Mixed literal and template sources
given: No GOLEMIC_DEV_TOKEN or GOLEMIC_REVIEWER_TOKEN env vars are set

acceptance_scenarios.title: Missing referenced env var produces error
given: Environment does not have MISSING_VAR set
No GOLEMIC_DEV_TOKEN or GOLEMIC_REVIEWER_TOKEN env vars are set
then: Error message does NOT contain any token value

acceptance_scenarios.title: Malformed template pattern produces error
given: No GOLEMIC_DEV_TOKEN or GOLEMIC_REVIEWER_TOKEN env vars are set
then: Error message contains description of malformed template (e.g., 'unclosed')
Error message does NOT contain the partial token value

acceptance_scenarios.title: Plain literals only — backward compatible
given: No GOLEMIC_DEV_TOKEN or GOLEMIC_REVIEWER_TOKEN env vars are set
No template references in the file

acceptance_scenarios.title: Empty env var with default fallback
given: Environment has EMPTY_VAR="" (set to empty string)
MISSING_VAR is not set at all
No GOLEMIC_DEV_TOKEN or GOLEMIC_REVIEWER_TOKEN env vars are set

test_cases: template.Resolver: plain literal returned unchanged with source file_literal
template.Resolver: ${VAR} resolves to env value with source template_env
template.Resolver: ${VAR:default} uses default when VAR unset with source template_default
template.Resolver: ${VAR:default} uses default when VAR is empty string
template.Resolver: ${VAR:} (empty default) resolves to empty string with source template_default
credentials.Loader: explicit GOLEMIC_DEV_TOKEN env overrides template file value
credentials.Loader: explicit env with empty string overrides template (results in missing error if other field also empty)
credentials.Loader: mixed literal + template fields both resolve correctly
credentials.Loader: old scaffold (empty literals) still produces missing credentials error
credentials.Loader: template error messages do not contain token values
credentials.Credentials: String() still shows ***set*** / ***unset*** for tokens regardless of source
preflight: existing credentials.json with literals is not overwritten (idempotency)
preflight: success Details includes source info like 'dev=template_env, reviewer=file_literal'
preflight: error Details never contains token values even when template error occurs
non_functional_requirements: Template resolution must not allocate excessively: single-pass regex or simple string scan
Error path must never include token values or env var values in any message
definition_of_done: All acceptance scenarios pass as automated tests
internal/template package has 100% test coverage of Resolver
internal/credentials tests cover all template resolution paths and error conditions
internal/preflight tests verify scaffold writes templates and checkCredentials includes source info
Existing tests continue to pass without modification (backward compatibility)
go test ./internal/... passes with zero failures
No token values or env var values appear in any test error output

decision_log.decision: Use ${VAR_NAME} and ${VAR_NAME:default} syntax, consistent with shell/CI conventions
decision_log.rationale: Familiar syntax reduces learning curve; ${} is not valid in GitHub tokens so no ambiguity

decision_log.decision: New internal/template package, separate from credentials to avoid circular deps and enable independent testing
decision_log.rationale: Credentials and preflight depend on each other indirectly via types; a standalone template package avoids any circular import risk

decision_log.decision: GOLEMIC_DEV_TOKEN / GOLEMIC_REVIEWER_TOKEN env vars always win, same as current behavior; they short-circuit template resolution
decision_log.rationale: Existing precedence is already tested and documented; template resolution only applies to file values, and env vars already override file values

decision_log.decision: Add private DevSource/ReviewerSource string fields with public accessor methods on Credentials struct
decision_log.rationale: Preflight needs to display where each token came from; source values are enumerable (file_literal, direct_env, template_env, template_default, unset)

decision_log.decision: New credentials.json scaffold writes ${GOLEMIC_DEV_TOKEN} and ${GOLEMIC_REVIEWER_TOKEN} instead of empty strings
decision_log.rationale: Most users will use the same env var names; the scaffold provides a working default that documents the env var names inline

decision_log.decision: template.Resolver interface with Resolve(value string) (string, string, error) returning (resolvedValue, source, error)
decision_log.rationale: Injectable interface enables testing with mock resolvers; source return value allows the caller to track resolution type without exposing resolver internals

decision_log.decision: Error messages may include env var names but never resolved token values or env var values
decision_log.rationale: Existing credentials tests already verify token values don't leak; the same constraint applies to template error paths

decision_log.decision: ${VAR:default} uses default when VAR is unset OR set to empty string; ${VAR:} explicitly allows empty token (which will then fail the existing missing-credentials check)
decision_log.rationale: Consistent with shell ${VAR:-default} semantics except the colon is mandatory; the explicit ${VAR:} case is useful for testing and edge cases

decision_log.decision: Existing literal values in credentials.json continue to work unchanged; old scaffold values (empty strings) still produce missing-credentials errors as before
decision_log.rationale: Literal values are not templates; empty strings resolve to empty and fail the existing token-presence check. No migration needed.

codebase_evidence.finding: Credentials struct has devToken and reviewerToken string fields with DevToken() and ReviewerToken() accessor methods. The struct currently has no source tracking. Loader.Load reads credentials.json, applies env var overrides, and validates both tokens are non-empty.

Project already uses text/template for prompt rendering, establishing a precedent for Go template usage. However, credentials template resolution is simpler (single value substitution) and a regex or string-scan based resolver in internal/template is more appropriate.

Preflight has checkCredentialsScaffolding and checkCredentials methods. checkCredentialsScaffolding calls createCredentialsSkeleton which writes the credentials.json file via preflight.credentialsSkeleton struct. checkCredentials loads credentials and validates tokens via gh api user.

Load method reads credentials.json with TOCTOU-safe open, parses with strict field checking, then applies GOLEMIC_DEV_TOKEN / GOLEMIC_REVIEWER_TOKEN env var overrides via os.LookupEnv. The override is per-token: env var wins if set (even to empty). Existing env vars short-circuit before any template resolution would occur.

Internal packages: agent, config, credentials, eventlog, preflight, prompt, repo, runner, worktree. No existing internal/template package. Preflight imports credentials and config. Credentials has no external imports beyond stdlib. Adding internal/template creates no circular dependency risk.

credentialsSkeleton struct mirrors credentials.credentialsFile with DevToken and ReviewerToken fields. createCredentialsSkeleton() writes it with empty strings. This is the code that must be changed to write ${GOLEMIC_DEV_TOKEN} and ${GOLEMIC_REVIEWER_TOKEN} templates.

checkCredentialsScaffolding creates credentials.json skeleton if missing. It is idempotent: never overwrites an existing file. This means existing users with literal tokens are never affected by the scaffold change.

Extensive test coverage including: valid file, env var overrides for each token, both env vars, env-only (no file), permission checks (0640, 0644), missing tokens, empty tokens, unrecognized JSON fields, malformed JSON, and token leak checks. All existing tests must continue to pass.
